# Research: live-counter-hud-surface

*Auto-generated by AIDLC research phase*

**Question:** Where can 'Money / Items Placed / Customers Active / Sales Today' live as a release-build HUD widget? DebugOverlay shows them today gated by OS.is_debug_build() at layer 100; the existing top-bar HUD does not. Map current data sources for each counter.

---

# Research: Live Counter HUD Surface

## Question Restated
Where can the four "live" counters — **Money**, **Items Placed**, **Customers Active**, **Sales Today** — live as a release-build HUD widget? They currently appear in `DebugOverlay` (gated by `OS.is_debug_build()`, `CanvasLayer` layer 100) but are not surfaced in the existing top-bar HUD. Map the data sources for each and recommend a placement.

---

## 1. Current Surfaces — What Exists Today

### 1.1 `HUD` (release, top bar) — `game/scenes/ui/hud.gd` + `hud.tscn`
- **CanvasLayer** at `layer = 10`.
- Always visible during `GameManager.State.GAMEPLAY`.
- Top bar (`HBoxContainer` named `TopBar`) currently contains, in order:
  1. `CashLabel` — `$X,XXX.XX`, animated count, pulse on delta.
  2. `TimeLabel` — `Day N — H:MM AM/PM`, phase color/tooltip.
  3. `SpeedButton` — Paused / Normal / Fast / Ultra.
  4. `ReputationLabel` — score + tier name + arrow flash.
  5. `CloseDayButton` — appended at runtime in `_create_close_day_button()`.
  6. `HubBackButton` — appended at runtime, hidden until inside a store.
- Other floating children (not in `TopBar`): `ObjectiveLabel`, `StoreLabel`, `MilestonesButton`, `SeasonalEventLabel`, `TelegraphCard`, `PromptLabel`, `ToastNotificationUI`, `InteractionPrompt`.
- **Money is already shown** as `CashLabel`. The other three counters are not surfaced.

### 1.2 `DebugOverlay` — `game/scenes/debug/debug_overlay.gd` + `debug_overlay.tscn`
- **CanvasLayer** at `layer = 100`, top-right anchored `Label`.
- `_ready()` calls `queue_free()` if `not OS.is_debug_build()` — so it cannot ship a release HUD.
- Toggled by `toggle_debug` action (F1).
- Built each `_process` from direct system pointers (`time_system`, `economy_system`, `inventory_system`, `customer_system`, `mall_customer_spawner`).
- Lines that contain the four counters of interest:
  - `"Cash: %s" % cash_text` → `economy_system.get_cash()` (already in HUD).
  - `"Customers: %s"` → `customer_system.get_active_customer_count()`.
  - `"Inventory — Backroom: %s | Shelf: %s"` → `inventory_system.get_backroom_items().size()` and `.get_shelf_items().size()`.
  - Sales-today is NOT actually rendered by `DebugOverlay` today; it would be `economy_system.get_items_sold_today()`.

So "Items Placed" maps to `inventory_system.get_shelf_items().size()` and "Sales Today" maps to `economy_system.get_items_sold_today()`. Both exist on the public API.

---

## 2. Data Source Map (per counter)

| Counter | Display | Authoritative source | Read API | Push signal | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Money** | `$1,234.56` | `EconomySystem` (`game/scripts/systems/economy_system.gd`) | `economy_system.get_cash()` / `player_cash` getter | `EventBus.money_changed(old, new)` | Already wired to HUD `CashLabel`. Per-store revenue available via `get_store_daily_revenue(store_id)` and `daily_financials_snapshot`. |
| **Items Placed** | shelf occupancy | `InventorySystem` (`game/scripts/systems/inventory_system.gd`) | `get_shelf_items().size()` (mall-wide) or `get_shelf_items_for_store(store_type).size()` (per store) | `EventBus.inventory_changed` (no payload), `EventBus.inventory_updated(store_id)`, `EventBus.item_stocked(item_id, slot_id)` | No "count" signal — recompute on `inventory_changed`. Cache is invalidated by `_invalidate_caches()`; `.size()` is O(1) once cache is warm. |
| **Customers Active** | live customer count in current store | `CustomerSystem` (`game/scripts/systems/customer_system.gd`) | `get_active_customer_count()` (in-store), plus `get_active_mall_shopper_count()` (mall hallway) | `EventBus.customer_entered(data)`, `EventBus.customer_left(data)` | Both signals are emitted from `spawn_customer()` / `despawn_customer()`. Increment/decrement counter on each. For "store + mall" combined view: sum `_active_customers.size() + _active_mall_shopper_count`. |
| **Sales Today** | items sold today | `EconomySystem` | `economy_system.get_items_sold_today()` (returns `_items_sold_today`) | `EventBus.item_sold(item_id, price, category)` increments it; `reset_daily_totals()` zeroes on new day | No dedicated "sales count changed" signal. Listen to `item_sold` (++) and `day_started` (= 0). |

### 2.1 Signals already connected by HUD (from `_ready()`)
`objective_text_changed`, `notification_requested`, `panel_opened/closed`, `reputation_changed`, `store_opened/closed`, `hour_changed`, `day_started`, `day_phase_changed`, `money_changed`, `speed_changed`, `seasonal_event_started/ended`, `event_telegraphed`, `random_event_telegraphed`, `locale_changed`, `build_mode_entered/exited`, `store_entered/exited`.

The new counters need three additional connections: `inventory_changed` (or `inventory_updated`), `customer_entered`, `customer_left`, `item_sold`. `day_started` is already connected — reuse for sales-reset.

---

## 3. Placement Options

Four candidate surfaces; trade-offs follow.

### Option A — Append to existing `TopBar` HBox (RECOMMENDED)
Add three new `Label`s as siblings of `CashLabel`/`TimeLabel`/`ReputationLabel`. Visually grouped with the cash counter so it reads as a single "stats strip."

**Pros**
- Zero new layout containers; reuses theme, separation, anchors.
- Money already lives here — co-locating its peer counters is the obvious mental model.
- Localized via existing `tr()` pattern; phase tinting / pulse animation patterns are reusable.
- Survives `panel_opened/closed` dimming logic uniformly with the rest of the bar.

**Cons**
- Top bar widens; risk of crowding at low resolutions. With current widths (`Cash:180`, `Time:200`, `Speed:80`, `Rep:200` ≈ 660px + separations 24×N), three new ~120px labels push the bar to ~1020px. Acceptable at 1280+ minimum.
- Mixing currency with raw integer counts could feel busy. Use icon glyphs (📦 / 👥 / 🛒) or short labels (`Placed:`, `Cust:`, `Sold:`) to disambiguate.

**Layout to add (in `TopBar`, between `TimeLabel` and `SpeedButton` so cash/time stay leftmost)**

```gdscript
[node name="ItemsPlacedLabel" type="Label" parent="TopBar"]
custom_minimum_size = Vector2(120, 0)
layout_mode = 2
size_flags_horizontal = 4
text = "Placed: 0"
horizontal_alignment = 1

[node name="CustomersLabel" type="Label" parent="TopBar"]
custom_minimum_size = Vector2(120, 0)
layout_mode = 2
size_flags_horizontal = 4
text = "Cust: 0"
horizontal_alignment = 1

[node name="SalesTodayLabel" type="Label" parent="TopBar"]
custom_minimum_size = Vector2(120, 0)
layout_mode = 2
size_flags_horizontal = 4
text = "Sold: 0"
horizontal_alignment = 1
```

### Option B — New `LiveCountersPanel` row beneath `TopBar`
A second `HBoxContainer` anchored just below the top bar (e.g., `offset_top = 48, offset_bottom = 76`).

**Pros**
- Clear visual separation of "session totals" (top) vs "live counts" (second row).
- Easier to hide/show as a unit (player toggle / settings flag).
- Doesn't crowd cash/time/rep.

**Cons**
- Eats vertical screen real estate near `ObjectiveLabel` (`offset_top = 88`) and `StoreLabel` (`offset_top = 52`) — would need offsets shifted.
- New container, new theming, new `panel_opened` dim hookup.
- Splits "money" from its sibling counters.

### Option C — Standalone `LiveCountersHUD` CanvasLayer
A separate scene at `layer = 11` (just above HUD), anchored top-right or bottom-left.

**Pros**
- Can be toggled independently (settings flag) without touching `hud.tscn`.
- Doesn't risk breaking existing top-bar tests.
- Conceptually closest to what `DebugOverlay` is today — promote it to a release surface.

**Cons**
- Another node to position, dim, localize, and keep in sync with build-mode/panel state.
- Duplicates the `_open_panel_count` dimming logic unless centralized.
- Overlaps with `SeasonalEventLabel` / `TelegraphCard` at top-right and `ObjectiveLabel` at top-center.

### Option D — Promote `DebugOverlay` itself
Drop the `OS.is_debug_build()` gate, keep it on layer 100, add a settings toggle (e.g., `Show Live Stats`). Strip the cheat-hint footer in release.

**Pros**
- Minimum churn — code already exists, polls `_process`, formats text.
- F1 toggle is already familiar to playtesters.

**Cons**
- Layer 100 sits above modals; will overlay open menus/dialogs — bad UX.
- Mixes cheat surface with player-facing HUD; future debug additions risk leaking.
- Plain monospace label doesn't match HUD theming.
- Would need to split cheat input handlers behind the build-flag while keeping the readout open in release — fiddly.

### Recommendation
**Option A** for first delivery. It is the lowest-risk, lowest-asset path: the data wiring is already in `EconomySystem` / `InventorySystem` / `CustomerSystem`; the HUD already listens on `EventBus`; and money is already there for visual continuity. Add a settings toggle later (`hud.show_live_counters`) if crowding becomes an issue, and only then consider migrating to Option B.

Keep `DebugOverlay` as-is for cheats; just remove the redundant readout lines once the HUD shows them, or leave them as a single-source debugging convenience (the overlay's value is the cheats, not the readout).

---

## 4. Concrete Implementation Plan (Option A)

### 4.1 Scene changes — `game/scenes/ui/hud.tscn`
Insert three new `Label` nodes inside `[node name="TopBar"]`, after `TimeLabel` and before `SpeedButton`. Use the layout snippet in §3 Option A.

### 4.2 Script changes — `game/scenes/ui/hud.gd`

**New constants**
```gdscript
const _COUNTER_PULSE_SCALE: float = 1.08
const _COUNTER_PULSE_DURATION: float = PanelAnimator.FEEDBACK_PULSE_DURATION
```

**New `@onready` refs (next to existing label refs)**
```gdscript
@onready var _items_placed_label: Label = $TopBar/ItemsPlacedLabel
@onready var _customers_label: Label = $TopBar/CustomersLabel
@onready var _sales_today_label: Label = $TopBar/SalesTodayLabel
```

**New state**
```gdscript
var _items_placed_count: int = 0
var _customers_active_count: int = 0
var _sales_today_count: int = 0
var _counter_pulse_tweens: Dictionary = {}  # Label -> Tween
```

**Wire signals in `_ready()`** (append to existing block)
```gdscript
EventBus.inventory_changed.connect(_on_inventory_changed)
EventBus.customer_entered.connect(_on_customer_entered)
EventBus.customer_left.connect(_on_customer_left)
EventBus.item_sold.connect(_on_item_sold)
# day_started already connected — extend handler to reset sales count.

_refresh_items_placed()
_refresh_customers_active()
_update_sales_today_display(0)
```

**Handlers**
```gdscript
func _on_inventory_changed() -> void:
    _refresh_items_placed()

func _refresh_items_placed() -> void:
    var inv: InventorySystem = _resolve_inventory_system()
    if inv == null:
        return
    var new_count: int = inv.get_shelf_items().size()
    if new_count == _items_placed_count:
        return
    var delta: int = new_count - _items_placed_count
    _items_placed_count = new_count
    _items_placed_label.text = tr("HUD_PLACED_FORMAT") % new_count
    _pulse_counter(_items_placed_label, delta > 0)

func _on_customer_entered(_data: Dictionary) -> void:
    _customers_active_count += 1
    _customers_label.text = tr("HUD_CUST_FORMAT") % _customers_active_count
    _pulse_counter(_customers_label, true)

func _on_customer_left(_data: Dictionary) -> void:
    _customers_active_count = maxi(_customers_active_count - 1, 0)
    _customers_label.text = tr("HUD_CUST_FORMAT") % _customers_active_count
    _pulse_counter(_customers_label, false)

func _refresh_customers_active() -> void:
    var cs: CustomerSystem = _resolve_customer_system()
    if cs == null:
        return
    _customers_active_count = cs.get_active_customer_count()
    _customers_label.text = tr("HUD_CUST_FORMAT") % _customers_active_count

func _on_item_sold(_item_id: String, _price: float, _category: String) -> void:
    _sales_today_count += 1
    _update_sales_today_display(_sales_today_count)
    _pulse_counter(_sales_today_label, true)

func _update_sales_today_display(count: int) -> void:
    _sales_today_label.text = tr("HUD_SOLD_FORMAT") % count
```

**Extend the existing `_on_day_started`**
```gdscript
func _on_day_started(day: int) -> void:
    _current_day = day
    _random_event_telegraph = ""
    _sales_today_count = 0
    _update_sales_today_display(0)
    _refresh_time_display()
    _refresh_items_placed()
    _refresh_customers_active()
```

**Pulse helper (mirrors `_pulse_cash_label`)**
```gdscript
func _pulse_counter(label: Label, positive: bool) -> void:
    PanelAnimator.kill_tween(_counter_pulse_tweens.get(label))
    var color: Color = (
        UIThemeConstants.get_positive_color() if positive
        else UIThemeConstants.get_negative_color()
    )
    var tween: Tween = PanelAnimator.pulse_scale(
        label, _COUNTER_PULSE_SCALE, _COUNTER_PULSE_DURATION
    )
    _counter_pulse_tweens[label] = tween
    PanelAnimator.flash_color(label, color, _COUNTER_PULSE_DURATION)
```

**System resolution** — HUD currently uses singletons (`GameManager`, `EventBus`, `ReputationSystemSingleton`). For one-shot "current value" reads at `_ready()` and after store changes, reach systems through `GameManager` (e.g., `GameManager.get_inventory_system()`, `.get_customer_system()`) — confirm the exact accessor names; if absent, use a `get_node_or_null("/root/InventorySystem")` lookup or wait for the first `inventory_changed` / `customer_entered` push and skip the initial snapshot.

### 4.3 Localization keys — `game/data/locale/en.csv` (and parallel `es.csv`)
```
HUD_PLACED_FORMAT,"Placed: %d"
HUD_CUST_FORMAT,"Cust: %d"
HUD_SOLD_FORMAT,"Sold: %d"
```
Spanish:
```
HUD_PLACED_FORMAT,"Colocados: %d"
HUD_CUST_FORMAT,"Clientes: %d"
HUD_SOLD_FORMAT,"Vendidos: %d"
```

The HUD already calls `_on_locale_changed`; ensure that handler refreshes the three labels (re-format via the `tr()` keys above) so live language switching works.

### 4.4 Per-store vs mall-wide semantics
- **In a single-store build**: `get_shelf_items().size()` and `get_active_customer_count()` are correct.
- **Hub/mall mode**: HUD listens on `store_entered`/`store_exited`. Decide:
  - **Default**: show counts for the active store (use `get_shelf_items_for_store(active_store_id)` and the in-store customer count).
  - **In hub** (no active store): hide the three labels, or show mall totals (`get_active_mall_shopper_count()` for customers; sum of shelf items across owned stores for placed; `economy_system.get_items_sold_today()` is already mall-wide).

Recommend: hide `ItemsPlacedLabel` and `CustomersLabel` while in hub (`store_exited` fired, `_hub_back_button.visible == true`), keep `SalesTodayLabel` always visible (it's already global). Show all three when inside a store.

### 4.5 Build-mode dim
Existing `_on_build_mode_entered` / `_on_build_mode_exited` apply `_BUILD_MODE_DIM_ALPHA` to the whole `HUD` CanvasLayer (or the `TopBar` — verify in the truncated portion). The new labels are children of `TopBar`, so they inherit dimming for free.

### 4.6 Modal panel handling
`_on_panel_opened_track` / `_on_panel_closed_track` increment `_open_panel_count`. The new labels do not need behavior changes; they already remain visible during modals like the rest of the top bar.

---

## 5. Edge Cases & Pitfalls

1. **`inventory_changed` fires often.** The handler does an O(N) shelf scan via the cached `_shelf_cache` (cache invalidated on every change, rebuilt on next `get_shelf_items()` call). For typical store counts (<200 items) this is negligible. If profiling flags it, debounce via a `Timer` (single-shot, 0.05s).
2. **`customer_entered` / `customer_left` payload is a `Dictionary`** with `customer_id`, `profile_id`, `profile_name`, `store_id`, `satisfied`, `reason`. We only need to ++/-- — ignore the dict. Watch out for `store_id` filtering if you go per-store: filter on `data["store_id"] == _active_store_id`.
3. **Initial state on save load.** After `deserialize`/scene load, `_ready` runs before any `customer_entered`. Always seed via `_refresh_customers_active()` from `CustomerSystem.get_active_customer_count()`. For sales today, `EconomySystem._items_sold_today` is restored by `_apply_state` — read it once on `_ready`: `_sales_today_count = economy_system.get_items_sold_today()`.
4. **Day rollover.** `EconomySystem._on_day_started` calls `reset_daily_totals()` which zeroes `_items_sold_today`. HUD's `_on_day_started` runs on the same signal — order is not guaranteed. Safer: in HUD's `_on_day_started`, set `_sales_today_count = 0` directly rather than re-reading from the system on this frame.
5. **Customer despawn during scene teardown.** Avoid going negative: `maxi(_customers_active_count - 1, 0)` is in the snippet above.
6. **Mall-shopper count.** `_active_mall_shopper_count` represents hallway traffic, not in-store. Don't add it to the store counter unless you intentionally want a "footfall" metric — call that out in the label if so (`Foot: %d`).
7. **Localization plurals.** `%d Customers` reads oddly at 1. If polish matters, use the existing `tr()` pattern with separate singular/plural keys (`HUD_CUST_ONE`, `HUD_CUST_MANY`) and a small helper.
8. **Width regressions.** Test at the project's minimum viewport (`Project Settings → Display → Window → viewport_width`). If at 1280 the bar wraps or clips, reduce `custom_minimum_size.x` to 90 and use icons.
9. **Test fixtures.** `hud.tscn` may be loaded by integration tests (`tests/`). Adding labels with `unique_name_in_owner` left at default is fine; existing `_objective_label` uses `unique_name_in_owner = true` — match the convention only if a test needs `%ItemsPlacedLabel` access.

---

## 6. Trade-off Summary

| Concern | Option A (TopBar) | Option B (2nd row) | Option C (own layer) | Option D (promote debug) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Asset/scene changes | Minimal (3 labels) | Medium (new HBox + layout shifts) | Medium (new scene + layer) | None (delete `queue_free`) |
| Visual coherence | High (matches cash) | Medium (clear grouping) | Low (floats apart) | Low (mono debug style) |
| Risk to existing UI | Low (Width only) | Medium (collides w/ Objective/Store labels) | Low | High (sits above modals) |
| Toggle/settings later | Easy (visible per label) | Easiest (whole row) | Easiest (whole layer) | Already toggled (F1) |
| Localization cost | 3 keys × N locales | Same | Same | Same; needs new theming |
| Per-store vs mall semantics | Easy via `store_entered/exited` | Easy | Easy | Already poll; harder to gate |

---

## 7. Acceptance Checklist

- [ ] `Placed: N` updates within 1 frame of `EventBus.inventory_changed`.
- [ ] `Cust: N` increments on every `customer_entered`, decrements on every `customer_left`, never goes negative.
- [ ] `Sold: N` increments on every `EventBus.item_sold` and resets to 0 on `day_started`.
- [ ] All three labels survive a save/load round-trip (initial seed from system getters in `_ready`).
- [ ] Labels respect `_BUILD_MODE_DIM_ALPHA` while in build mode.
- [ ] Spanish locale keys present; `_on_locale_changed` re-renders the three labels.
- [ ] HUD layout does not clip at minimum supported viewport width.
- [ ] `DebugOverlay` cheats (`Ctrl+M`, `Ctrl+C`, `Ctrl+H`, `Ctrl+D`) drive the new HUD counters correctly when triggered (good smoke test — confirms the signal path end-to-end).
- [ ] Integration tests that load `hud.tscn` still pass; new nodes don't break `unique_name_in_owner` lookups elsewhere.
